stories.title: Witt Jr. Grade 1 MCL Sprain — 'Best Case Scenario' for Royals
stories.angle: Bobby Witt Jr. received MRI results and was diagnosed with a Grade 1 MCL sprain in his right knee — the "best case scenario" per manager Matt Quatraro. Grade 1 MCL sprains typically heal in 1-3 weeks. The Royals will wait until Sunday to evaluate Witt's status. The All-Star shortstop hit a home run before exiting Thursday's 14-6 win over the Cardinals. His All-Star candidacy and the Royals' playoff picture hang in the balance.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
WITT UPDATE ✅

Bobby Witt Jr. has a Grade 1 MCL sprain in his right knee.

Manager Quatraro: "Best case scenario."

Grade 1 = typically 1-3 weeks to heal. Royals evaluate Sunday before any IL decision.

The good news is real — the worry isn't gone yet. ⚾

#Royals #BobbyWittJr

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Grade 1 MCL sprain for Bobby Witt Jr.

Best case scenario. 1-3 week timeline. Sunday evaluation.

He still hit a HR before leaving Thursday. The man plays through everything. 💪

Royals fans, breathe. #Royals

facebook.long_form:
Royals fans got the news they were hoping for on Saturday morning.

Bobby Witt Jr. has a Grade 1 MCL sprain in his right knee — and manager Matt Quatraro called it exactly what it is: "best case scenario."

Witt exited Thursday's 14-6 win over the Cardinals in the fourth inning with right knee discomfort. He had already hit a home run before leaving. An MRI Saturday morning showed the sprain, and medical guidance puts Grade 1 MCL sprains in the 1-3 week recovery window. The knee can typically heal on its own without surgery.

The Royals aren't rushing. They plan to wait until Sunday to evaluate how Witt feels before deciding whether to place him on the injured list or keep him day-to-day. The All-Star Game is July 14 — right in that 1-3 week window from Sunday's decision point.

The news isn't all-clear, but it's as close to it as anyone could realistically hope for after watching him exit early. Grade 1 is the mildest MCL classification. The concern is real, but so is the optimism. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: Witt Jr. has a Grade 1 MCL sprain — "best case scenario," per the Royals. Now they wait until Sunday. ⚾ #Royals

stories.title: Ranger Suarez Takes No-Hitter Into 7th — Red Sox Top Mariners 6-2
stories.angle: Boston Red Sox lefthander Ranger Suarez carried a no-hitter into the seventh inning Friday night in Seattle before Josh Naylor's one-out double ended the bid. Suarez struck out five and walked three in 6⅔ innings, earning his first win since April 27 as Boston beat the Mariners 6-2. The 30-year-old lefty retired the first 10 batters he faced before a fourth-inning walk. A near-historic outing that was still one of the best starts of the 2026 season.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Ranger Suarez took a no-hitter into the 7th inning last night in Seattle.

Josh Naylor's double ended it. Final: 6⅔ IP / 1 H / 5 K / 3 BB.

Red Sox win 6-2. First Suarez win since April 27. ⚾

The bid ended — but what a night. 🔥

#RedSox #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Friday night in Seattle, Ranger Suarez was three outs from history.

The Boston Red Sox lefthander carried a no-hitter into the seventh inning at T-Mobile Park before Josh Naylor hit a one-out double to break up the bid. Suarez had retired the first 10 batters he faced, walked Josh Naylor's teammate in the fourth, and otherwise dominated a Mariners lineup that couldn't touch him for six-plus innings.

His final line: 6⅔ innings, one hit, five strikeouts, three walks. The Red Sox won 6-2.

It was Suarez's first win since April 27. The 30-year-old lefty has had a difficult stretch, but Friday night looked like a different pitcher — aggressive, commanding, keeping Seattle off-balance from the first batter to the last.

The no-hitter didn't happen. But what Suarez did Friday night was still one of the best single-game pitching performances of 2026. And sometimes that's enough. 🔥
facebook.image_caption: Suarez took a no-hitter into the 7th in Seattle. Naylor's double ended it. Red Sox win 6-2. 🔥 #RedSox

stories.title: Schwarber's Historic HR Pace — Still No. 1 at 25 With Alvarez Chasing
stories.angle: Kyle Schwarber leads MLB with 25 home runs and an extraordinary season pace. His 25 HRs make him the first player to reach that mark in 2026. He is on pace for approximately 65+ home runs — which would rank third-highest in MLB history behind Bonds (73, 2001) and McGwire (70, 1998). The Home Run Derby is July 13 at Citizens Bank Park, Schwarber's home stadium. Alvarez is the closest pursuer. History is in play.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber: 25 HR.

First player to reach 25 in 2026. Pace: ~65+ HR for the season.

That would rank 3rd in MLB history.

Only Bonds (73, 2001) and McGwire (70, 1998) have done it higher.

Oh, and the Home Run Derby is at HIS ballpark on July 13. 🔥

#Phillies #Schwarber

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
The HR race is ON. 🏆

Schwarber: 25 HR (MLB lead)
Alvarez: chasing

Home Run Derby at Citizens Bank Park (Schwarber's house) in 23 days.

This is going to be fun. ⚾ #MLB #HomeRunDerby

facebook.long_form:
Let's stop and appreciate what Kyle Schwarber is doing in 2026.

Twenty-five home runs. The first player in baseball to get there this season. And when you do the math on a 162-game pace, the number that comes out the other end lands in historically rare territory — approximately 65+ home runs for the full year.

That would place Schwarber's 2026 season third on the all-time single-season home run list, behind only Barry Bonds (73, 2001) and Mark McGwire (70, 1998).

It's June. There's half a season left. Schwarber has done this before — he's always been a home run hitter — but this version of him is operating at a different level.

And here's the thing that makes the next month appointment television: the Home Run Derby is July 13, and it's at Citizens Bank Park. Schwarber's home. His fans. His field. The MLB HR leader getting to swing in front of his own crowd at his own ballpark.

Yordan Alvarez is chasing. The race is real. And the Derby is in 23 days. 🔥
facebook.image_caption: Schwarber at 25 HR, on pace for 65+. The Derby is at his home park July 13. This is appointment baseball. 🔥 #Phillies

stories.title: All-Star Ballot — Ohtani Leads at 1.165M, Phase 1 Closes June 25
stories.angle: Shohei Ohtani leads all of baseball with 1,165,133 votes in the first All-Star ballot update. Yordan Alvarez leads the AL at 1,015,768. Aaron Judge at 977,460. Phase 1 voting closes June 25 at noon ET — five days away. The top two vote-getters at each position and top six outfielders advance to Phase 2 (opens June 29). All-Star Game is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
2026 MLB All-Star ballot — with 5 days left until Phase 1 closes:

🔵 Ohtani leads all: 1,165,133 votes
🔴 Alvarez leads AL: 1,015,768 votes
⚾ Judge: 977,460 votes

Phase 1 closes June 25 at noon ET. Game is July 14 in Philadelphia.

Who are you voting for? 🗳️

#MLB #AllStar2026

facebook.long_form:
Five days left in Phase 1 of MLB All-Star voting — and the leaderboard looks like you'd expect, with one name towering above everyone else.

Shohei Ohtani leads all of baseball with 1,165,133 votes. Not just the DH category. Not just the National League. All of baseball.

Yordan Alvarez tops the American League at 1,015,768 votes. Aaron Judge sits at 977,460. There are real races at multiple positions — Ben Rice has quietly built a strong case for the AL first base spot with Aaron Judge sidelined. In the NL, Freddie Freeman leads with 870,606 votes at first base over Matt Olson's 802,848.

Phase 1 voting closes June 25 at noon ET. The top two vote-getters at each position and the top six outfielders advance to Phase 2, which opens June 29. The All-Star Game is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia.

With the Derby also at Citizens Bank on July 13, Philly fans have a week of baseball history coming their way. Get your votes in. 🗳️
facebook.image_caption: Ohtani leads All-Star voting at 1.165M. Phase 1 closes June 25. Five days left. Who are you voting for? 🗳️ #MLB

stories.title: Saturday MLB — Mets at Phillies on Fox, Witt Update Sunday
stories.angle: Saturday's featured Fox game: New York Mets vs. Philadelphia Phillies at Citizens Bank Park (7:15 PM ET). Phillies 9 GB in NL East (Braves 46-27 lead the division). Cleveland at Houston also on Fox (7:15 PM ET). Royals off Saturday — Sunday brings Witt's next evaluation. Braves lead NL, Ohtani-led Dodgers chasing in NL West. Weekend slate to watch.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Saturday night baseball on Fox 📺

🎯 Mets vs Phillies — 7:15 PM ET
🎯 Cleveland vs Houston — 7:15 PM ET

Schwarber (25 HR) in the lineup. The Derby host plays in his house tonight.

What are you watching? ⚾

#MLB #FoxSaturdayBaseball

facebook.long_form:
Saturday baseball is here — and tonight's national game might be the most interesting one of the week.

Fox Saturday Baseball features the New York Mets at the Philadelphia Phillies at 7:15 PM ET, with Kyle Schwarber (25 HR, MLB leader, Home Run Derby host) in the middle of the Phillies lineup.

Also on Fox: the Cleveland Guardians at the Houston Astros at 7:15 PM ET.

The Phillies are 30-28, chasing in the NL East behind the Braves (46-27). A win Saturday matters. A Schwarber home run at Citizens Bank Park would matter more.

And while all this is happening, the Royals have the day off — giving Bobby Witt Jr. a chance to rest the Grade 1 MCL sprain in his right knee. The Royals plan to evaluate Witt on Sunday and decide whether he goes on the injured list or stays day-to-day.

Grab the remote. Saturday night baseball is appointment television. ⚾📺
facebook.image_caption: Mets at Phillies on Fox tonight at 7:15 PM ET. Schwarber (25 HR) takes the field. What more do you need? ⚾ #MLB
